Subject: 🎮 Chaotic Suns — Beta Test Invitation

Hey {{name}},

I've been building a game called Chaotic Suns — a cosmic survival sim where you guide a civilization orbiting multiple unpredictable suns. Think orbital physics meets civilization management, with a dash of existential dread.

It's in beta and I'd love your feedback. Takes about 10-15 minutes to get into it.

WHAT IT IS:
• N-body physics simulation — three suns doing their chaotic dance
• Civilization management — population, knowledge, era shifts (Stable vs Chaotic)
• Tech tree — 10 technologies to research, from basic survival to N-body solution
• Space fleet — build and launch 4 types of probes to explore the system
• Prediction mode — draw orbital paths and get accuracy scores
• Random events — solar flares, gravitational spikes, meteor showers
• 4 scenarios, 4 difficulty levels, 10 achievements

WHAT I NEED FROM YOU:
• Play for at least 15 minutes
• Try the prediction mode (E key) and tech tree (T key)
• See how far you can push your civilization
• Report any bugs, crashes, or confusing UX
• General vibe check — is it fun? What sucks?

DOWNLOAD (Linux):
https://github.com/armstrongsam25/chaotic-suns

To run:
  1. Extract the zip
  2. python3 -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
  3. pip install -r requirements.txt
  4. python3 main.py

(Windows/Mac: same steps, just use python/python3 as appropriate)

CONTROLS:
  Space     Pause/Resume
  E         Prediction Mode
  H         Help (full controls)
  T         Research Lab
  1-4       Launch probes
  Arrows    Pan
  +/-       Zoom
  Esc       Menu/Quit
  F5/F9     Quick save/load

Reply here with any feedback — good, bad, ugly, all of it.

Thanks!
Sam
